nativeRange.habitat: Coral-rich areas of shallow lagoons and seaward reefs, typically at depths of 0–30 m, where the ranges of the two parent species overlap.

diet.notes: Has been documented accepting spirulina, marine algae (nori), high-quality angelfish preparations, mysis shrimp, and frozen meaty foods. This hybrid tends to require a notably algae-rich diet, consistent with both parent species. Regular feeding 2–3 times daily is generally advised.

summary: A rare natural hybrid between the Lemonpeel Angelfish (C. flavissima) and the Halfblack Angelfish (C. vroliki), with variable coloration blending yellow and dark posterior markings. Semi-aggressive; generally considered reef-safe with caution.
careNotes:
The Lemonpeel Hybrid Angelfish is a naturally occurring interspecific hybrid between Centropyge flavissima (Lemonpeel Angelfish) and Centropyge vroliki (Halfblack/Pearlscale Angelfish), found where the ranges of both parent species overlap, notably in the Marshall Islands. Coloration is variable and intermediate between the two parents — typically a partially yellow body with some of the dark posterior pigmentation characteristic of C. vroliki. It is commonly described as a rare find in the aquarium trade.

Care requirements are generally similar to both parent species. A well-established aquarium with abundant live rock for grazing is generally advised. It has been known to nip at small-polyped stony corals and clam mantles, so it is best described as reef-safe with caution. Best kept singly; introducing other dwarf angelfish of similar genus or coloration tends to result in aggression. As a dwarf angelfish hybrid, it is likely a protogynous hermaphrodite (female-first sex changer), consistent with both parent species. In the wild, parent species tend to form harems (one dominant male with multiple females).

Temperature note: LiveAquaria recommends 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C) for the hybrid specifically. FishBase data for the parent species records 24–28°C (C. flavissima) and 25.6–29.3°C (C. vroliki). ReefApp data for C. flavissima cites 27°C as optimal. These values were averaged (min: avg of 22.2+24+25.6 ≈ 24°C; max: avg of 25.6+28+29.3 ≈ 27.5°C). The spread is moderate; aquarists should target 24–26°C for routine keeping.

Tank size note: LiveAquaria specifies 70 US gallons (265 L) minimum. ReefApp data for the C. flavissima parent cites 300 L. A minimum of 300 L is used here, biased toward the larger value given the species' territorial nature. 300 L is the nearest nice-list value above the 282 L average.
breedingNotes: No specific captive breeding reports for this hybrid have been identified; as an interspecific hybrid it is expected to follow the general Centropyge pattern of protogynous hermaphroditism and broadcast spawning. Hybridization of the parent species has been documented in the wild where ranges overlap. Captive breeding of the parent species is already considered advanced; the hybrid is likely equally or more challenging.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for parent-species cross-verification. FishBase records C. flavissima at max 14 cm TL, temperature 24–28°C, family Pomacanthidae, order Acanthuriformes. No FishBase entry exists for the hybrid itself.

additional.name: FishBase (C. vroliki)
additional.notes: Used for parent-species cross-verification. FishBase records C. vroliki (= C. vrolikii) at max 12 cm TL, temperature 25.6–29.3°C, native range Western Pacific including Marshall Islands. Confirms hybrid origin zone.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia (C. flavissima)
additional.notes: Third source for parent-species parameters. Cites max 14 cm, optimal temp 27°C, minimum tank 300 L. Used to triangulate temperature and tank size for the hybrid entry.

fish.breedingNotes: No captive breeding reports confirmed for this interspecific hybrid. Expected to follow parent-species pattern: protogynous hermaphrodite, broadcast spawner. Captive rearing is likely advanced to not-achievable outside specialist facilities.
